llcFilesWith: Hawaii Department of Commerce and Consumer Affairs, Business Registration Division (Application for Registration of Trade Name, Form T-1)
soleProprietorFilesWith: Hawaii Department of Commerce and Consumer Affairs, Business Registration Division (the same statewide trade name registration applies to individuals and sole proprietors)
feeBasis: The Business Registration Division fee to register a trade name is $50 (non-refundable), and renewal is also $50.
renewalRule: A registration is effective for five years and may be renewed for successive five-year periods by filing within six months before expiration with a $50 fee (HRS § 482-3).
protectionNote: A Hawaii trade name registration is constructive notice statewide of the registrant's claim while the name stays in actual use (HRS § 482-3), and registered trade names are among the records that block a later substantially identical entity name. Registration does not by itself grant ownership, which is acquired through adoption and bona fide use.
searchNote: Registered trade names appear in the Department of Commerce and Consumer Affairs business search.
notes: Hawaii registers trade names statewide with the Department of Commerce and Consumer Affairs, Business Registration Division (HRS ch. 482). LLCs and sole proprietors alike file Form T-1 for $50; the registration lasts five years and renews for $50. Registration is constructive notice of the claim and blocks a later substantially identical entity name, but it does not by itself grant ownership, which comes from actual use.

sources.title: Trade Names, Trademarks and Service Marks — Registration; term (5 years); constructive notice

sources.citation: Hawaii Department of Commerce and Consumer Affairs
sources.title: Trade Name, Trademark, or Service Mark registration (Form T-1, $50, 5-year term)
